params.command: string — absolute path to executable (e.g., powershell.exe). host-integration resolves this via process.env on Windows; the now-playing-source supplies the program name and host-integration locates it.

fields.onStartup: function(cb: () -> Promise<void>) -> void — register a startup hook; called once after plugin process boot and host registration
fields.onShutdown: function(cb: () -> Promise<void>) -> void — register a shutdown hook; called when the host signals the plugin to exit

notes: now-playing-source uses spawnSidecar to start the SMTC reader process (PowerShell with the Windows.Media.Control WinRT assembly load, per probe-ip2 findings). host-integration is the only section that calls Node.js's child_process; now-playing-source delegates to it so the source remains testable without an actual host present. host-integration's log() routes messages through the Stream Dock host's logging channel (events-sent.html#logMessage) so they appear in the host's plugin log.
